1.1: Paul, an Apostle of Yeshua The Messiah in the will of God, to those who are in Ephesaus, holy and faithful in Yeshua The Messiah.
1.2: Peace with you and grace from God our Father and from our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
1.3: Blessed is The God and The Father of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, he who has blessed us with all the spiritual blessings in Heaven in The Messiah.
1.4: Just as he chose us beforehand in him from before the foundation of the universe, that we shall be holy and without spot before him, and he ordained us beforehand in love for himself.
1.5: And he has constituted us as children by Yeshua The Messiah, as that is pleasing to his will,
1.6: That the glory of his grace may be praised, that which overflows upon us by his Beloved One.
1.7: For in him we have redemption, and the forgiveness of sins by his blood, according to the riches of his grace,
1.8: That which has superabounded in us in all wisdom and in all intelligence.
1.9: And he has taught us the mystery of his will, that which he had before ordained to perform in himself,
1.10: For the administration of the end of time, that all things which are in Heaven and in Earth would be made new again by The Messiah.
1.11: And we are chosen by him as he had before ordained us and willed to do all according to the counsel of his will,
1.12: That we would be those who first hoped in The Messiah, to the honor of his glory.
1.13: For you also heard by him the word of truth, which is The Good News of your salvation, and you believed in him, and you were sealed in The Spirit of Holiness who was promised,
1.14: Who is the pledge of our inheritance for the redemption of those who are living and for the glory of his honor.
1.15: Because of this, behold, I also, from when I heard of your faith in our Lord Yeshua The Messiah and your love for all The Holy Ones,
1.16: I do not cease to give thanks for you and to remember you in my prayers,
1.17: So that The God of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, The Father of glory, would give you The Spirit of wisdom and revelation with his knowledge,
1.18: And that the eyes of your hearts would be enlightened, that you will know what is the hope of his calling and what is the wealth of the glory of his inheritance in The Holy Ones,
1.19: And what is the excellence of the greatness of his power in us, by those things which we believe, according to the action of the immensity of his power,
1.20: Which he performed in The Messiah and raised him from among the dead and set him at his right hand in Heaven,
1.21: Higher than all Principalities, Rulers, Powers, and Dominions, and higher than every name that is named, not only in this universe, but also in the one that is coming.
1.22: And he has put everything into subjection under his feet, and he has given him who is higher than all as The Head of the church,
1.23: Which is his body and the fullness of him who fills all in all.

2.1: And he fills even you who were dead in your sins and in your stupidity.
2.2: And these things in which you walked from the first, you were in them according to the secular life of this world and according to the will of The Ruler of The Authority of The Air and of this spirit which is diligent in the children of disobedience.
2.3: We also were employed in those works from the first in the desires of our flesh, and we were doing the will of our flesh and of our minds, and we were entirely children of rage, as the rest.
2.4: But God who is rich in his compassion, because of his great love with which he loved us,
2.5: When we were dead in our sins, he gave us life together with The Messiah, and by his grace he saved us.
2.6: And he has raised us up with him and seated us with himself in Heaven in Yeshua The Messiah,
2.7: To show the coming ages the greatness of the riches of his grace and his sweetness, which has come upon us by Yeshua The Messiah.
2.8: For it is by his grace that we have been saved through faith, and this faith was not from you, but it is the gift of God,
2.9: Not of works, lest anyone should boast.
2.10: For we are his creatures who are created in Yeshua The Messiah for good works, those things which God had from the first prepared that we should walk in them.
2.11: Because of this, be mindful that you Gentiles from the first were of the flesh, and that you were called uncircumcision by that which is called circumcision and is a work of the hands in the flesh.
2.12: And you were at that time without The Messiah, and you were aliens from the government of Israel and strangers to The Covenant of The Promise and you were without hope and without God in the world.
2.13: But now you are in Yeshua The Messiah, when from the first you were distant, and you have come near by the blood of The Messiah.
2.14: For he is our peace who made the two one, and he destroyed the wall that was standing in the middle.
2.15: And he has canceled the hatred by his flesh and the law of commands in his commandments, that for the two, he would create in his Person one new man, and he has made peace.
2.16: And he has reconciled the two with God in one body, and in his crucifixion he has killed the hatred.
2.17: And he came preaching The News of Peace to you, to the distant ones and to those who are near,
2.18: Because in him, we both have access by One Spirit to The Father.
2.19: Therefore, you are not strangers, neither guests, but inhabitants of the city of The Holy One and children of the household of God.
2.20: And you are built up upon the foundation of the Apostles and of the Prophets, and he is The Head of The Corner of the building-Yeshua The Messiah.
2.21: And the whole building is constructed by him and grows into a holy temple in THE LORD JEHOVAH,
2.22: While you also are built by him for the dwelling of God in The Spirit.

3.1: Because of this, I Paul am the prisoner of Yeshua The Messiah for the sake of you Gentiles,
3.2: If you have heard of the administration of the grace of God, which is given to me among you.
3.3: For the mystery was taught me by revelation, just as I have written to you briefly.
3.4: As whenever you read, you can understand my knowledge in the mystery of The Messiah,
3.5: Which in other generations was not made known to the children of men as now it is revealed to his holy Apostles and to his Prophets by The Spirit:
3.6: That the Gentiles would be heirs and members of his body and of The Promise that was given in him by The Gospel,
3.7: That of which I have been a minister, according to the gift of the grace of God which is given to me by the action of his power;
3.8: To me, I who am the least of all The Holy Ones, this grace has been given, that I should preach The Good News among the Gentiles, the unsearched wealth of The Messiah,
3.9: And that I may enlighten every person by the administration of the mystery which was hidden from the world in God The Creator of all,
3.10: That by the church would be made known the full-diverse wisdom of God to Principalities and to Rulers who are in Heaven,
3.11: That which he had prepared from eternity and performed in Yeshua The Messiah Our Lord,
3.12: Him by whom we have boldness and access in the confidence of his faithfulness.
3.13: Because of this I request that you shall not grow weary of me by my afflictions which are for your sakes, for this is your glory.
3.14: And I bow my knees to The Father of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah,
3.15: Him from whom every fatherhood is named, that is in Heaven and in Earth,
3.16: That he would grant you according to the riches of his glory to be confirmed by power in his Spirit, that in your inner person,
3.17: The Messiah may dwell by faith, and in your hearts by love, when your root and your foundation shall be confirmed,
3.18: That you can discover with all the holy, what is the height and depth and length and breadth,
3.19: And you may know the magnitude of the knowledge of the love of The Messiah and you may be filled in all the fullness of God.
3.20: But to him who is more than almighty to do for us and is greater than what we ask or imagine, according to his power that is active in us,
3.21: To him be glory by his church in Yeshua The Messiah to all generations of the eternity of eternities. Amen.

4.1: I who am a prisoner in our Lord request of you, therefore, that you walk just as it is suitable to the calling in which you are called,
4.2: In all humility of mind and quietness and patience, and that you would be patient one toward another in love,
4.3: And to be diligent to keep the harmony of The Spirit in the bonds of peace,
4.4: And that you would be in one body and One Spirit as when you were called in one hope of your calling.
4.5: For THE LORD JEHOVAH is One, and the faith is one, and the baptism is one,
4.6: And One is God The Father of all, and over all, and with all and in us all.
4.7: But to each one of us grace is given according to the size of the gift of The Messiah.
4.8: Therefore it is said, “He ascended to the heights and took captivity prisoner and he has given gifts to the children of men.”
4.9: But that he ascended, what is it but that also he first descended to the lower regions of The Earth?
4.10: He who descended is the same who also ascended higher than all Heavens to restore all things.
4.11: And he gave some who are Apostles and some who are Prophets and some who are Evangelists and some who are Pastors and some who are Teachers,
4.12: For the perfecting of The Holy Ones, for the work of the ministry, for the building up of the body of The Messiah,
4.13: Until we all shall be one entity in the faith and in the knowledge of The Son of God and one perfect man with the dimensions of the stature of the maturity of The Messiah,
4.14: Neither shall we be children who are shaken and troubled for every wind of crafty teaching of the children of men, those who plot to seduce by their cunning.
4.15: But we have been firm in our love that all our affairs may increase in The Messiah, who is The Head,
4.16: And from him, the whole body constructed and joined in every joint, according to a gift given in measure to every member for his growth of the body, that his building may be completed in love.
4.17: But I say this and testify in THE LORD JEHOVAH, that from now on, you should not be walking as others of the Gentiles who walk in the emptiness of their minds,
4.18: And they are dark in their intellects and are aliens to the Life of God, because there is no knowledge in them and because of the blindness of their heart,
4.19: Those who have cut off their hope and handed themselves over to lewdness and to the cultivation of every impurity in their lust.
4.20: But you have not learned The Messiah in this way,
4.21: If truly you have heard him and you have learned of him, as whatever is truth is in Yeshua.
4.22: But you should put aside from you your first way of life, that old man, which is corrupted by deceitful desires,
4.23: And you should be made new in the spirit of your minds.
4.24: And you should put on the new man, who has been created by God in righteousness and in the purity of the truth.
4.25: Therefore, put aside from you lying and let a man speak the truth with his neighbor, for we are members one of another.
4.26: Be angry and do not sin, and do not let the sun set on your anger.
4.27: Neither should you give place to The Slanderer.
4.28: But whoever has stolen, let him not steal from now on, but let him toil with his hands and produce good that he might have to give to the one who is in need.
4.29: Let not any hateful words come out of your mouth, but whatever is good and useful for improvement that you may give grace to those who hear,
4.30: Neither be grieving the Holy Spirit of God, for you have been sealed in him for the day of redemption.
4.31: Let all bitterness, fury, rage, clamor, and insults be taken away from you with all wickedness,
4.32: And be sweet one toward another and affectionate, and be forgiving one another, just as God in The Messiah has forgiven us.

5.1: Be therefore imitators of God, as beloved children,
5.2: And walk in love, as also The Messiah has loved us and handed himself over for our persons, the offering and sacrifice to God, for a sweet fragrance.
5.3: But fornication, all impurity and greed are not even to be named at all among you, as is appropriate for Holy Ones,
5.4: Neither abusive language, neither worthless words, nor of disgrace, nor of nonsense- these things that are unnecessary, but in place of these, thanksgiving.
5.5: You have known this, that no person who is a fornicator or impure or a greedy person ( who is an idol worshipper ) has any inheritance in The Kingdom of The Messiah and of God.
5.6: Let no one deceive you with empty words, for it is because of these things the anger of God comes on the children of disobedience.
5.7: You should not therefore be partners with them.
5.8: For you were formerly darkness, but now you are light in our Lord, therefore so walk as children of Light.
5.9: For the fruits of The Light are in all goodness, righteousness and truth.
5.10: Distinguish what is pleasing before Our Lord.
5.11: Neither should you fellowship with the Servants of darkness, because they have no fruit in them, but you should rebuke them.
5.12: For the thing that they do in secret is an abomination to mention.
5.13: For all things are corrected by The Light and they are revealed, and everything that reveals is light.
5.14: Therefore it is said, “Awake, you who sleep, and arise from among the dead and The Messiah will illuminate you.”
5.15: See therefore that you walk honorably, not as the fools, but as the wise,
5.16: Who redeem their opportunities because the days are evil.
5.17: Therefore do not be stupid, but understanding what the will of God is.
5.18: Neither be drunk with wine in which is debauchery, but be filled with The Spirit.
5.19: But speak to yourselves in Psalms and hymns and songs of The Spirit; be singing in your hearts to THE LORD JEHOVAH.
5.20: Be giving thanks always for the sake of every person in the name of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah to God The Father.
5.21: Be subject to one another in the love of The Messiah.
5.22: Wives, be subject to your husbands as to Our Lord,
5.23: Because the man is the head of the woman just as The Messiah is also The Head of the church and he is The Savior of the body.
5.24: But just as the church is subject to The Messiah, in this way also wives are to their husbands in all things.
5.25: Husbands, love your wives, as The Messiah also loves his church and gave himself up for her sake,
5.26: To sanctify and purify her in the washing of water and in the word.
5.27: And he shall establish the church for himself without blemish or wrinkle, neither any such things whatsoever, but it will be holy and without defect.
5.28: In this way men ought to love their wives as their bodies. Whoever loves his wife loves himself.
5.29: For no man ever hated his own body, but he nourishes and cares for his own, just as The Messiah does also for his church.
5.30: Because we are members of his body and we are of his flesh and of his bones.
5.31: Therefore, a man should leave his father and his mother and should cleave to his wife, and the two of them shall be one flesh.
5.32: This is a great mystery, but I am speaking about The Messiah and about his church.
5.33: Moreover, you also, everyone of you should in this way love his wife as himself; but the woman should revere her husband.

6.1: Children, obey your parents in Our Lord, for this is right.
6.2: And this is the first commandment of promise: “Honor your father and your mother,
6.3: And it shall be well for you and your life shall be long on The Earth.”
6.4: Parents, do not anger your children, but rear them in the discipline and in the teaching of Our Lord.
6.5: Servants, obey your Masters who are in the flesh with reverence and trembling and with simplicity of heart, as unto The Messiah,
6.6: Not publicly, as if you please the children of men, but as Servants of The Messiah, doing the pleasure of God.
6.7: And serve them with all your soul in love, as unto our Lord and not as unto men,
6.8: As you know that anything a man does which is excellent will be repaid by Our Lord, whether he is a Servant or a Freeman.
6.9: Also you Masters, in this way do to your Servants; be forgiving offenses unto them, because you know that your Master also is in Heaven, and there is no favoritism with him.
6.10: Therefore, my brethren, be strengthened in our Lord and in the force of his power.
6.11: And put on all the armor of God, so that you can stand against the strategies of The Devil.
6.12: Because your fight has not been with flesh and blood, but with Principalities and The Rulers and The Powers of this dark world and with wicked spirits which are under Heaven.
6.13: Because of this, put on all the armor of God that you shall be able to confront The Evil One, and when you are ready in all things, you shall stand.
6.14: Stand therefore and gird your waist with the truth and put on the breastplate of righteousness,
6.15: And shoe your feet with the readiness of The Gospel of peace,
6.16: And with these, take to you the shield of faith, that with it you may have the power to quench all of the blazing bolts of The Evil One.
6.17: Put on the helmet of salvation and grasp the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
6.18: Pray with all prayers and with all desires always in The Spirit and be watching with him in prayer every moment as you pray constantly and make supplication for the sake of all The Holy Ones,
6.19: Also for me, that the word may be given to me by opening my mouth, that publicly I may preach the mystery of The Gospel,
6.20: That of which I am an Ambassador in chains, that I may speak it with boldness as it is necessary for me to speak it.
6.21: But so that you will know also each of my concerns and whatever I do, behold, Tukiqos, a beloved brother and trustworthy minister in our Lord will inform you.
6.22: For I shall send him to you for this purpose, that you may know my affairs, and he will comfort your hearts.
6.23: Peace be with our brethren and love with faith, from God The Father and from our Lord Yeshua The Messiah.
6.24: Grace be with all those who love our Lord Yeshua The Messiah without corruption. Amen.
